name: Ascend 910B3
_v3_calibration_note: Per-opcode cycle costs from CCE microbenchmarks on triton_profiling (CANN 8.5). PIPE_V opcodes: 40-block × 2000-iter × 128-SIMD, Python timing via torch.npu.synchronize(). PIPE_S scalar ALU from CCE v1 (P_scalar_add_sustained 0.60 GFLOPS). MTE from CCE v1 bandwidth benchmarks. Replace all previous per-pipe multiplier calibrations.

inter_path.description: Cube and Vector paths execute in parallel

intra_path.description: All units within each path can execute in parallel (pipelined)

calibration.description: v3 calibration: per-opcode cycle costs from CCE microbenchmarks on Ascend 910B3 (CANN 8.5, triton_profiling container). Validated against chunk_kda (MIX_AIC, 4096 blocks) and split_qkv (AIV-only, 20 blocks) E2E profiling. Chunk E2E ratio improved from 0.12 (raw DES) to 0.63 (v3). Split E2E ratio improved from 0.023 to 0.17.

startup_latencies.notes: MTE3 startup=40 verified via chunk profiling (PIPE_MTE3 ratio=1.1 with this value). pipe_barrier=350 estimated from chunk PIPE_S profiling residual. Others are datasheet seeds pending validation.

vector_op_cycles_per_vec_instruction._description: Per-PIPE_V-opcode cycle cost. Measured on Ascend 910B3 via CCE microbenchmarks: 40 blocks × 2000 iterations × 128-element SIMD, Python timing with torch.npu.synchronize(). Dependent-chain latency measurement (includes store + loop overhead as constant per kernel).

_measured_batch1_20260625.method: 40 blocks, 2000 iterations, 128 SIMD, 3 repeats, dependent-chain latency

_measured_batch2_20260625.method: same as batch1, separate execution for vdiv/vand/vor/vnot/varange
_measured_batch2_20260625.note: vdiv uses fp32 (div is fp32-only on this hardware)

_notes_by_opcode.vmul_vadd_vsub: Simple ALU: ~3-3.5 cyc/128elem. Fastest vector ops — fully pipelined.
_notes_by_opcode.vmax_vmin: Same ALU class as vmul — estimated same cost.
_notes_by_opcode.vdiv: fp32 divide — ~5.2 cyc. Uses different microcode from multiply.
_notes_by_opcode.vbrc: Broadcast/fill — 14.6 cyc. Much more expensive than ALU. Includes zero-init + fill sequence.
_notes_by_opcode.vcast: Type cast (fp32→fp16) — 10.2 cyc. Format conversion overhead.
_notes_by_opcode.vcmp: Compare + where — 5.5 cyc. Moderate cost, includes mask + select.
_notes_by_opcode.vsel: Select/mux — 4.5 cyc. Slightly cheaper than compare.
_notes_by_opcode.vand_vor_vnot: Bitwise — 3-5 cyc. On integer ALU path, similar to basic arithmetic.
_notes_by_opcode.varange: Range generation — 9.9 cyc. Includes arange + cast sequence.
_notes_by_opcode.vexp_vlog_vsqrt_vrsqrt: Transcendental — 82.6 cyc. From CCE v1 (P_vector_exp_sustained 3.33 GFLOPS). MUCH slower than ALU ops — avoid in hot paths.

vector_op_cycles_per_vec_instruction.notes_legacy_keys: Keys like 'simple_ops_add_sub_mul_etc', 'exp', 'div' are the original key names read by HardwareConfig.cpp parseJSON(). New opcode-specific keys (vbrc, vcmp, etc.) need corresponding readInt() calls in the C++ parser. Until then, the post-processor (perfbound/calibration/des_trace_postprocessor.py) applies these values.

scalar_op_cycles._description: Per-PIPE_S-opcode cycle cost. Scalar operations are 1-wide (single ALU), fundamentally serial.

scalar_alu._description: Scalar arithmetic/logic ALU. CCE v1: P_scalar_add_sustained 0.60 GFLOPS → 3.1 cyc/op.

agu._description: Address Generation Unit — pointer arithmetic, memref shape ops. TBD: CCE benchmarks.
agu.source: Estimated from profiling residual. Needs dedicated CCE benchmarks.

sync._description: Synchronization barriers. Dominate wall time in scalar-heavy kernels despite small op count.
sync.source: Estimated from chunk_kda profiling residual (pipe_barrier). Others proportional to expected hardware sync protocol cost.

misc.source: Zero-cost for compile-time ops. Min/max estimated as AGU.

cube_mac_cycles._description: Cube MAC pipeline model. Deep pipeline (91 stages). issue_interval=1 cycle per MAC, latency=92 cycles for dependency tracking.

mmadL1.source: CCE_v1_P_cube_fp16_sustained 5.16 TFLOPS. Chunk profiling confirms issue_interval≈1 (cube_mac ratio=0.89).

mte_cycles._description: MTE data movement cost = startup_cycles + transfer_bytes × cycles_per_byte.

PIPE_MTE3.source: CCE_v1_BW_gm_to_l1 141 GB/s. Startup=40 verified by chunk (ratio=1.1).

scalar_overhead.verified_by: chunk_kda profiling: AIC scalar ratio=0.035 (28.4× gap), AIV scalar ratio=0.258 (PIPE_S dominates 84% of AIV wall time). Confirms 1-wide scalar ALU.
scalar_overhead.impact: For kernels with >10% scalar ops (PIPE_S), scalar ALU becomes the E2E bottleneck. This is a HARDWARE LIMIT, not a modeling error.

parallelism.description: Core counts from Ascend 910B spec. Block overhead = 0.12 us/block verified by chunk_kda: (111,892 - 111,384) / 4096 = 0.12 us.

e2e_formula.v3_validated: E2E = max(waves_AIC × per_block_AIC_cycles, waves_AIV × per_block_AIV_cycles) / (clock × 1000) + grid_blocks × block_launch_overhead_us
e2e_formula.chunk_kda_verification: E2E_model = max(205 × 47.1us, 103 × 682.7us) = 70,315 us. Real = 111,892 us. Ratio = 0.63 (up from 0.12 with raw DES).

per_pipe_v3_accuracy.PIPE_MTE3: raw=0.028 → v3=1.430 (slightly over, sync overhead not modeled)
per_pipe_v3_accuracy.PIPE_MTE2_V: raw=0.014 → v3=0.781 (57x, MTE model is excellent)

split_qkv.note: PIPE_S sync ops (barrier/wait_flag) still use estimated values. Real barrier cost likely higher than 250-500 cyc. This is the primary remaining gap.

remaining_gap_sources: PIPE_S sync ops (barrier/wait_flag/set_flag) — estimated 100-500 cyc each. Actual may be 2-3× higher. These dominate PIPE_S wall time in chunk (1,191,314 / 1,350,088 = 88% of all cycles).
DES pipeline scheduling — DES models pipes as parallel but real hardware may have port contention when multiple pipes issue to shared units.
Loop overhead in split_qkv — 103 token iterations amplify per-iteration overhead (barrier sync between MTE→Vector→Scalar per iteration).
